Arizona Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Start with the only questions that matter for travelers: does your current license already cover Arizona, what does endorsement cost, and how quickly can you get cleared to work?

Last verified 2026-02-25 Official board No Dedicated State Licensing Board
No Interstate Compact Decision point for travel-ready licensing

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Arizona

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Arizona. To work here, plan on a full endorsement or transfer application through No Dedicated State Licensing Board.

Good news: the exact cost, documentation, and board path are laid out below so there is no guesswork.

Before You Start

  1. Open the official board portal before gathering documents.
  2. Budget the endorsement fee and expected processing window below.
  3. Assume you need full approval before starting work unless the board says otherwise.
Endorsement Fee $0 State-level transfer cost
Processing Time N/A (No state application) Best estimate from current board data
Fingerprinting Not clearly required Background screening expectation
Temporary License Not listed Useful if you need a faster start date
See Endorsement Steps Official Board Portal

Endorsement Application Steps

Work through these in order. This sequence is the fastest way to move from out-of-state license to a board-cleared application in Arizona.

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete 1,000 to 1,200 hours of supervised practice through a dietetic internship.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians ($200 fee).

  3. Maintain CDR Registration

    Maintain your CDR credential by completing 75 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every five years and paying the CDR annual maintenance fee.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$0 (State Fee)
Processing time
N/A (No state application)
Renewal fee
$0 (State Fee)
Renewal cycle
No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours.

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Card: Your active CDR card serves as your primary credential in Arizona.
  • ACEND Verification Statement: Proof of completion of your accredited degree program and supervised practice.

Board Contact

Board
No Dedicated State Licensing Board
Department
Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.
Phone
N/A
Email
Visit Official Board Portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $0 (State Fee). Renewal cycle: No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours..

Arizona Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

Does Arizona require a state dietitian license?

No. Arizona does not mandate state-specific licensure for dietitians. Your national CDR registration as a Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) is the recognized credential.

What does it cost to practice as a dietitian in Arizona?

There is no state application or renewal fee. The CDR national exam costs $200, and the annual CDR maintenance fee is $80 (increasing to $90 in 2027).

Is there pending legislation to require state licensure in Arizona?

As of 2026, Arizona does not have an active, enacted state licensure law for dietitians. Legislative efforts (such as H.B. 2820 from 2021) have been introduced in the past.

Does Arizona have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Arizona?

Most endorsement applications take N/A (No state application). If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
